# betterhook

> A fast, memory-tight, worktree-native git hooks manager built for the era of parallel AI coding agents. A single static Rust binary with line-streaming subprocess I/O, a per-worktree dispatch wrapper in the shared `.git/hooks/` directory, an opt-in coordinator daemon that serializes tool conflicts across worktrees, and NDJSON output for agent harnesses.

This file lists every page in the betterhook documentation. Each entry links to the published Mintlify page; the Markdown source for each page is at the same URL with `.md` appended.

## Get started

- [Introduction](https://betterhook.dev/introduction): What betterhook is, why it exists, and the parallel-agent workload it targets.
- [Quickstart](https://betterhook.dev/quickstart): From zero to a working worktree-aware pre-commit hook in 60 seconds.
- [Why betterhook](https://betterhook.dev/why-betterhook): Side-by-side comparison with lefthook, husky, and pre-commit, including the specific lefthook bugs betterhook fixes.

## Essentials

- [Installation](https://betterhook.dev/essentials/installation): Install the binary and set up hook wrappers; coexisting with husky / pre-commit.
- [Configuration](https://betterhook.dev/essentials/configuration): Every field of `betterhook.toml` with realistic examples and a full schema reference.
- [Templates and globs](https://betterhook.dev/essentials/templates): `{staged_files}`, `{push_files}`, `{all_files}`, `{files}` and the include / exclude pipeline.
- [Isolation](https://betterhook.dev/essentials/isolation): Tool mutexes, sharded semaphores, per-worktree `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` injection.
- [Extends and local overrides](https://betterhook.dev/essentials/extends): Sharing a base config across repos and layering a per-machine override.
- [Config formats](https://betterhook.dev/essentials/formats): TOML vs YAML vs KDL vs JSON — picking one, and how mixed-format `extends` works.
- [Monorepo support](https://betterhook.dev/essentials/monorepo): `[packages.<name>]` blocks with path filters and per-package hook inheritance.

## Commands

- [Commands overview](https://betterhook.dev/commands/overview): Every betterhook subcommand at a glance.
- [betterhook init](https://betterhook.dev/commands/init): Scaffold a starter `betterhook.toml`.
- [betterhook install](https://betterhook.dev/commands/install): Write worktree-aware hook wrappers into the shared `.git/hooks/`.
- [betterhook uninstall](https://betterhook.dev/commands/uninstall): SHA-verified wrapper removal that never touches user-modified hooks.
- [betterhook run](https://betterhook.dev/commands/run): Run a hook directly. Supports `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--skip`, `--only`.
- [betterhook status](https://betterhook.dev/commands/status): JSON snapshot of installed hooks, config, and worktree identity.
- [betterhook explain](https://betterhook.dev/commands/explain): Print a job's resolved plan (command, files, env, lock) without executing.
- [betterhook fix](https://betterhook.dev/commands/fix): Run every job's `fix = ...` variant — the agent self-correction surface.
- [betterhook cache](https://betterhook.dev/commands/cache): Inspect, clear, or verify the content-addressable hook cache.
- [betterhook builtins](https://betterhook.dev/commands/builtins): List and inspect the 12 bundled linter / formatter wrappers.
- [betterhook doctor](https://betterhook.dev/commands/doctor): Pre-flight health check for install, config, tools, cache, and watcher.
- [betterhook import](https://betterhook.dev/commands/import): Convert lefthook, husky, hk, or pre-commit configs into `betterhook.toml`.
- [betterhook migrate (deprecated)](https://betterhook.dev/commands/migrate): Hidden alias for `betterhook import --from-format lefthook`.

## Agent integration

- [Agent integration overview](https://betterhook.dev/agents/overview): Why betterhook was built agent-first and what that buys you.
- [NDJSON output](https://betterhook.dev/agents/ndjson): The stable event schema for `betterhook run --json`.
- [Self-correction loop](https://betterhook.dev/agents/self-correction): The canonical run → fix → run pattern.
- [Conductor integration](https://betterhook.dev/agents/conductor): Running betterhook across multiple agent worktrees in Conductor.

## Architecture

- [Architecture overview](https://betterhook.dev/architecture/overview): How betterhook is organized and how a commit flows through it end to end.
- [Worktree model](https://betterhook.dev/architecture/worktree-model): The git primitives betterhook relies on (`--git-dir`, `--git-common-dir`, `--show-toplevel`).
- [Runner](https://betterhook.dev/architecture/runner): The Tokio executor, output multiplexer, cancellation model, and git-index lock.
- [Coordinator daemon](https://betterhook.dev/architecture/daemon): The opt-in daemon — Unix socket, lock registry, idle-exit lifecycle, flock fallback.
- [Capability DAG](https://betterhook.dev/architecture/capability-dag): The read / write / network / `concurrent_safe` declarations that drive the dependency graph.
- [Content-addressable cache](https://betterhook.dev/architecture/cache): blake3-keyed cache that turns a clean re-commit into a sub-100 ms replay.
- [Speculative execution](https://betterhook.dev/architecture/speculative): Prewarming `concurrent_safe` jobs into the cache as files are saved.

## Migration guides

- [From lefthook](https://betterhook.dev/migration/from-lefthook): Convert a `lefthook.yml` to `betterhook.toml` in one command.
- [From husky](https://betterhook.dev/migration/from-husky): Drop `.husky/*` shell scripts and move them into `betterhook.toml`.
- [From pre-commit](https://betterhook.dev/migration/from-pre-commit): Move from `.pre-commit-config.yaml` to `betterhook.toml`.

## Reference

- [Exit codes](https://betterhook.dev/reference/exit-codes): The stable exit-code contract every betterhook subcommand honors.
- [Environment variables](https://betterhook.dev/reference/env-vars): `BETTERHOOK_SKIP`, `BETTERHOOK_ONLY`, `BETTERHOOK_NO_LOCKS`, `BETTERHOOK_DAEMON_SOCK`, and what's deliberately not used.
- [Wire protocol](https://betterhook.dev/reference/protocol): The bincode IPC format for direct integration with the coordinator daemon.
- [Performance targets](https://betterhook.dev/reference/performance): Cold start, idle RSS, runner RSS, scaling targets, and how to measure locally.

## Changelog

- [Changelog](https://betterhook.dev/changelog): Release history, following Keep a Changelog and semver.

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/leonardomso/betterhook): Source code, issues, contributing guide.
- [Issues](https://github.com/leonardomso/betterhook/issues): File a bug or request a feature.
